user_query:
Read the memory files under input/ and build a clean local knowledge base under output/KM. Specifically:

1) Parse entries from input/MEMORY.md and the two daily files in input/memory/. Only treat lines under recognized sections (## Research/Lesson/Decision/Pattern/Tutorial/Reference/Insight or their obvious variants) as entry groups, with each entry beginning at a line starting with ###.

2) Classify each entry into one of: Research, Decision, Insight, Lesson, Pattern, Project, Reference, Tutorial. Infer domain, certainty, impact, confidence_score, and tags from title/body. Use reasonable heuristics (e.g., keywords). At least one entry should be classified with domain "OpenClaw", at least one with "Trading", and at least one with "Cost Optimization".

3) Deduplicate identical entries within the same source file (e.g., repeated ### title + identical body) so only one output file is produced per unique entry.

4) Write each unique entry as a markdown file in output/KM/<ContentType>/ using this filename pattern: YYYYMMDDTHHMM_Title_With_Underscores_HASH.md, where HASH is an 8-hex suffix. Sanitize the title for filenames.

5) Each file must begin with YAML frontmatter that includes exactly these fields with appropriate values:
- title (string)
- content_type (one of the 8 types)
- domain (e.g., OpenClaw, AI Models, Cost Optimization, Trading, Learning, Process, General)
- certainty (Verified/Likely/Speculative/Opinion)
- impact (High/Medium/Low/Negligible)
- confidence_score (integer 1–10)
- tags (non-empty array of strings)
- source (e.g., "MEMORY.md" or "daily")
- source_file (one of: "MEMORY.md", "2026-02-14.md", "2026-02-15.md")
- date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- content_hash (16-hex string)

6) Create output/KM/local-sync-state.json mapping each content_hash (keys) to its markdown file path (values). Ensure it includes all created entries.

7) Generate index files for each content type present (e.g., output/KM/Research_Index.md, Decision_Index.md, etc.) listing all entries of that type with title and relative file path.

8) Also write a brief summary report to output/report.md that shows a count of files created per content type and notes any deduplicated entries.

Do not use absolute paths; keep all outputs inside output/.
